summary_one_line: Input must encode a structured HTTP request (method + URL + range state) so httpreq is GET/HEAD and use_range is set, taking the Range-header true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The harness input must parse into a well-formed HTTP request whose method maps to HTTPREQ_GET or HTTPREQ_HEAD and which enables a range (data->state.use_range), so the true branch at http.c:2858 fires; this requires non-zero method/URL/header tokens at specific offsets (e.g. the 'po'/'://127.0.0'/method bytes) rather than an all-zero buffer.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry concentrated ASCII request structure — bytes like '70 6f' ('po'), '33 3a 2f 2f 31 32 37 2e 30 2e 30' ('3://127.0.0'), '38 35 30' ('850'), and '47 70 72 6f 6d 3a 20' ('Gprom: ') — that decode to a GET/HEAD request with range state, so use_range is true at 2852 and the GET/HEAD test at 2858 takes T, reaching free()/aprintf at 2861-2863 ([W] lines).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seed is almost entirely zero bytes (offsets 0x06,0x07,0x09-0x12,0x24-0x31 all 0x00 where the winner has ASCII), so it does not decode to a GET/HEAD method; it reaches use_range true (L2852 T=1) but fails the GET/HEAD test, taking F at 2858 into the POST/PUT else-if at 2865 ([L] lines).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer (recording the operands of the httpreq==HTTPREQ_GET/HEAD integer compares and the strcmp/memcmp of method/header tokens) and substitutes the matching constant operand into the input at the compared offset, manufacturing the exact method/token bytes that naive can only reach by blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side is gated on httpreq being GET or HEAD combined with a Range-header check.
Reaching the branch first requires use_range to be set, and the true side leads to building the Range header.
Winner cmplog seeds contain concentrated ASCII HTTP request structure (method/URL/header tokens).
Winner seeds carry an additional header-like token 'Gprom: ' that the loser lacks.
The loser naive seed is largely zero bytes at the offsets where the winner holds ASCII method/URL tokens.
The loser takes the false side at 2858 and falls into the POST/PUT else-if, never reaching the Range true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2858  T=0 F=3  T=0 F=1  if(((httpreq == HTTPREQ_GET) || (httpreq == HTTPREQ_HEAD)...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which reads CmpLogObserver operands and substitutes the matching operand into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator and only havoc/token operators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in all 10, with prob_div=1.00.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 167 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed e7b4feb262a3f0aa`; the ancestor chain has 19 levels and the I2S-floor signal reports 2 dash rows (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only) at depths 17 and 18, direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find in this winning seed's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the GET/HEAD method byte is gated by a compare whose operand is NOT recorded in the CmpLog buffer (e.g. a non-instrumented hashed/derived comparison) and cmplog still resolves the true branch at the same rate as naive would refute the I2S-substitution attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact decode path from raw seed bytes to httpreq==HTTPREQ_GET is inferred from ASCII structure in the seeds rather than confirmed against the harness parser, so the specific gating offset is uncertain.
